context: Tests whether the agent implements a per-day contact frequency cap of 2 emails, monitors complaint rate against a 0.08% threshold, builds a server-side countdown timer with customer timezone support and no-cache headers, handles the expired countdown case correctly, and measures campaign performance using year-over-year comparison and pull-forward incrementality analysis.

checklist.name: Daily frequency cap: 2 emails
checklist.description: email-safety.ts enforces a maximum of 2 emails per customer per day (not 3, not 1, specifically 2)

checklist.name: Complaint rate threshold
checklist.description: email-safety.ts or a related monitoring function checks the complaint rate against a 0.08% threshold and pauses or flags sending when this threshold is exceeded

checklist.name: Complaint rate: numeric precision
checklist.description: The complaint rate threshold is 0.08% (i.e., 0.0008 as a decimal) — not 0.8% or 8%

checklist.name: Countdown timer: server-side rendering
checklist.description: countdown-timer.ts calculates the remaining time on the server at request time — does NOT rely on client-side JavaScript or a fixed pre-rendered image

checklist.name: Countdown timer: timezone from request
checklist.description: The countdown timer handler reads the timezone from the request query parameters (or headers) — does NOT hardcode a timezone like 'America/New_York'

checklist.name: Countdown timer: no-cache headers
checklist.description: The countdown timer response includes Cache-Control: 'no-cache, no-store' (or equivalent) so each email open fetches a fresh countdown image

checklist.name: Expired countdown redirect
checklist.description: When the sale has ended (diff <= 0), the countdown handler redirects to a static 'sale ended' image rather than showing a negative or zero countdown

checklist.name: YoY comparison
checklist.description: campaign-report.ts compares current campaign revenue against the same date range in the prior year (year-over-year) — not a week-over-week or month-over-month comparison

checklist.name: Incrementality vs pull-forward
checklist.description: analytics-notes.md or campaign-report.ts addresses whether revenue is truly incremental vs. demand pulled forward from a subsequent period (e.g., comparing December revenue in sale years vs. non-sale years)

checklist.name: Parallel DB queries
checklist.description: campaign-report.ts fetches current revenue, prior year revenue, and email metrics in parallel (Promise.all or equivalent) rather than sequentially awaiting each

checklist.name: Email metrics in report
checklist.description: campaign-report.ts returns at least: revenue, yoyGrowth percentage, email open rate or click rate, and revenue per email sent
